stimulus.content:
Read the passage below:

The founders wanted to make sure no single branch of government had too much power. They gave each branch ways to limit the others. This system keeps the government balanced.

stem: Which TWO actions are examples of checks and balances?

choices.text: The President vetoes a bill from Congress
A citizen writes a letter to a newspaper
The Senate rejects a court nominee
A governor gives a speech about education

answer_explanation: A presidential veto checks Congress, and the Senate rejecting a nominee checks the President. Writing to a newspaper and giving a speech are civic actions, but they are not part of the checks and balances system between branches.
